topic: A Mongolian horseman using an Uurga to catch a wild horse, wide landscape shot.

parsed_generation.script:
Shot 1 [00:00-00:02]: wide: A Mongolian horseman on the steppe rides with an Uurga toward a distant wild horse across a wide landscape.
Shot 2 [00:02-00:05]: side-angle: The rider swings the Uurga, rope arcing toward the wild horse in the open grasslands.
Shot 3 [00:05-00:08]: close-up: The Uurga loop tightens around the wild horse as the rider stabilizes the catch.
parsed_generation.narration: Across eight seconds, a lone Mongolian horseman on the steppe uses the Uurga to rope the wild horse, culminating in a secure catch on the broad landscape.

feedback: The sequence stays centered on the Mongolian horseman and the uurga catching a wild horse, but the timestamp structure and shot cadence differ from the gold standard (shorter first cut, offset second/third). To more closely match, structure the script into two 4-second segments with explicit large-scale wide landscape setup and a later, precise shot of the loop engaging the horse, plus a closing moment of the catch. Enhance imagery with tangible details (dust on the steppe, horsehair, wind) and align camera cues (Wide landscape, High-angle, Close-up on the loop) to mirror the gold standard’s cinematic rhythm.
strengths: Clear shot-by-shot progression with defined camera angles (wide, side-angle, close-up).
Uses action-first, cinematic language to describe the uurga interaction.
fixes: Adopt the gold standard's two 4-second segments and ensure the phrasing mirrors the exact sequence: 1) wide landscape with the rider approaching; 2) the uurga extends toward the wild horse.
Increase visual specificity by mentioning textures and environment (dust, grass, horse tack) and tighten the action to emphasize the capture moment.
